title: Agentic Workflows Are Overhyped
compiled_truth:
Agentic workflows overhyped is the contrarian take that needs more airtime. The hype: a constellation of LLM agents calling each other, each specialized, each fully autonomous, solves arbitrary problems by emergent coordination. The reality: most production-grade 'agentic workflows' are actually deterministic pipelines with one or two LLM calls and a lot of conventional code holding them together.

This essay argues agentic workflows are overhyped on three axes. (1) Reliability: more agents means more places for hallucination to compound. (2) Debuggability: when an 8-agent system fails, the trace is unreadable. (3) Cost: each agent call is independent, no batching, no caching ... the bill is brutal.

The alternative is fat code thin harness: encode the workflow in deterministic skills, use the LLM only at decision points where genuine reasoning is needed. Less agentic, more workable.
timeline: - **2026-04-06** | First draft. Got accused of 'gatekeeping AI' by a reviewer.
